other_observers: Brandon Percival and Paul Hurtado; Brandon Percival first found this bird the sometime during the week before this date.
specific_location: adjacent to Pueblo Lake State Park's South Shore Marina
description: This gull was distinctly larger than a Ring-billed Gull and smaller than Herring Gull with direct comparisons available. A frosty looking gull with gleaming white underwing. Head someone small and pigeon-like, whitish with brown streaks. Bill appearing mostly blackish with some pinkish showing through the black at the base of the "lower mandible". Bill looked relatively short compared to Herring Gull. Iris color dark. In my opinion there was some dark smudging posterior to the eye, a character more consistent with Thayer's than will Kulien's Gull. Enitre body, including primaries and tail appeared apporoximately the same color, a light tan. Tertials barred light brown, NOT COMPLETED DARK, as would be expected for Kumlien's and not as expected for Thayer's Gull. Primaries long, extending well past the tail, with noticeable white edging to both inner and outer edges. Undertail coverts whitish with large light brown circular spots. Feet and legs pinkish.Behaviors: Mostly at rest on ice. No specific behaviors exhibited.Call: none heardPlumage: first winter
